feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Update control of movement type is incorrect (entry 601 X X B L _ E)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone !!

While performing PGI in T Code VL02N I am getting the below error -
Update control of movement type is incorrect (entry 601 X X B L _ E)
Message No. M7226

I tried to fix this in OMJJ - But it doesn't allow to add or copy new entries (Can someone confirm if its authorization issue or SAP behavior)

As per few SAP notes I also tried maintaining the entry in SM30 --> V_156X_VC, but it didnt help. 

Can someone give a strong resolution.

Thanks & Regards,
Mohit S Rohera

entry.title: Hiding Condition type
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Our client's requirements is to not to show the inventory price of the Material to the Salesman. While in the Sales quotation and Sales order Pricing procedure the PCIP Condition type displays the Internal Price which is used to calculate the Profit margin of the Material. This Profit margin value is used in the SAP Standard sales reports because of this I can't remove this condition type from the Pricing procedure, so i want to hide this conditon type in the screen without removing its function in the Sales quotation and Sales order. Please provide me the possible way to achive this requirement or suggest any workaround for this requirement.

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: Error during Dhareeba upload via BTP – Custom PO Document Type not accepted
entry.content:
While executing the Dhareeba upload program via SAP BTP, the system fails during Purchase Order creation when using a custom PO document type.

The error is observed in the API response captured via browser DevTools → Network tab.

Error Message (from API response) XML<error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata">
<code>API_MM_PUR_PO/064</code>
<message xml:lang="en">Use purchase order type "Standard" ("NB") or a type copied from "NB".</message>
<innererror/>
</error>
Show more lines Observed Behavior

  • The API rejects the custom PO document type.
  • The system explicitly restricts usage to:
    • Standard PO type NB, or
    • Document types copied from NB.

Expected Behavior

  • The system should allow processing of custom PO document types configured in the system (including those not directly copied from NB), or
  • Documentation/clarification should

entry.title: Unable to access BI reports via LPD_CUST configuration after upgrade to S/4HANA 2023.
entry.content: Hi Team, We are facing an issue after upgrading our system from S/4HANA 2021 to S/4HANA 2023. Before Upgrade (Working Scenario): - We were accessing BI (external system) reports from Fiori Launchpad. - Configuration was done using: • Application Type: SAP Fiori App using LPD_CUST • Transaction: LPD_CUST - A Launchpad Role (e.g., ZSAP_BI) and Instance (ANALYTICS) was created. - URLs for BI reports (BOE OpenDocument links) were maintained in LPD_CUST. - Corresponding Target Mapping was created in Fiori Launchpad Designer. - When users clicked the tile: → System redirected to BI system → User entered credentials → Report opened successfully After Upgrade (S/4HANA 2023): - The same configuration is either not working or not supported. - We understand that Launchpad Designer and LPD_CUST based integrations are deprecated/obsolete. - We are unable to identify the correct approach/tool to reconfigure this setup. Current Requirement: We need to enable Fiori tiles to launch external BI reports (BOE URLs) similar to the previous setup.

entry.title: FIori ID:F1079（PIR更新） アプリが開けない
entry.content: FIori ID:F1079（PIR更新） を開いた際に「ターゲットが見つかりません。Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getMetadata') 」というメッセージが表示され、アプリを利用することができない。

entry.title: \/N/UI2/FLPD_CUST is not opening the launchpad designer but routing to launchpad url.
entry.content:
When we try to execute /n/ui2/flpd_cust to access the fiori designer , it routing back to launchpad url.But when we access it via url its loading.

 

https://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/arsrvc_upb_admn/main.html?scope=CUST&sap-client=<xxx>&sap-l...

 

Kindly let us know the steps to access it via transaction.

entry.title: MBP- Disable Default setting in ALL the reports and enable User-Specificsetting in ALL the reports
entry.content:
Hi SAP team,

We have some users can change the ALV layout in the ME23n in the PO details which cause the default value altered and sometimes missing information. Kindly assist us on how to check and solve this issue as we have performed some test but it still failed based on some reference of old ECC system

entry.title: Cross-Company Code Payments & BRS: Standard OBYA vs. Duplicating House Banks?
entry.content:
Message Body:

Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for some validation on the best practice for a cross-company code payment scenario we are facing in our current S/4HANA implementation for a client in Qatar.

The Business Requirement

We have 5 company codes (1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 5000). Frequently, a payment needs to be made for an invoice sitting in one company code (e.g., 2000), but the funds will actually be disbursed from the bank account of another company code (e.g., 1000) due to balance availability.

The Current Configuration (The BRS Problem)

To achieve this, my senior configured the physical bank account (e.g., Al Rayyan Bank, Account No: 123456789) across all 5 company codes.

  • Because SAP does not allow duplicate bank account numbers for the same bank key, a workaround was used by appending the company code to the account number (e.g., 123456789-1000, 123456789-2000, etc.).

  • Main, Incoming, and Outgoing GLs were created in all company codes for this single physical bank account.

The Issue: This breaks our Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS). If a user selects the house bank in company code 2000 or 3000 to make a payment, the transactions sit in the GLs of those respective company codes. However, we receive only one consolidated physical bank statement from the bank. Reconciling one statement against GLs scattered across five different company codes is essentially impossible.

My Proposed Solution

Instead of duplicating the House Bank, I am planning to use the standard Cross-Company Code transaction process.

  1. House Bank Setup: Maintain the physical House Bank and its associated bank GLs only in the actual paying company code (e.g., 1000).

  2. OBYA Configuration: Configure OBYA (Cross-Company Code Clearing) between all necessary company code pairs using a dedicated Intercompany Receivable/Payable GL.

  3. Payment Process: When paying a CC 2000 invoice via F-53, the user starts in CC 1000, selects the 1000 Bank GL, and processes the open item for CC 2000. SAP automatically clears the invoice in 2000, posts the payment in 1000, and generates the OBYA intercompany postings.

The Complication: Document Splitting & Profit Centers

Here is where I need the most guidance. Document Splitting is active in our system.

  • Currently, Profit Centers are created 1:1 with Plants and are company-code specific (e.g., CC 1000 has PC 1100, CC 2000 has PC 2100).

  • Under my proposed OBYA solution, when paying a CC 2000 invoice from a CC 1000 bank account, Document Splitting will inherit the profit center from the vendor invoice (PC 2100) onto the CC 1000 bank line item.

  • To prevent system errors, I would need to extend all Profit Centers to all Company Codes. ### My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is the OBYA cross-company payment approach the standard way to resolve the BRS issue in this scenario?

  2. Regarding Document Splitting: Is extending all profit centers to all company codes the correct approach to handle the inheritance during cross-company payments? Will this create downstream reporting issues (e.g., trial balance or segment reporting conflicts), and if so, how do you typically mitigate them?

Any insights or guidance would be highly appreciated!

Best Regards,

Syamlal VS

SAP FICO Consultant

entry.title: Cross-Company Code Payments, BRS, & Document Splitting: Standard OBYA vs. Duplicating House Banks?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for some validation on the best practice for a cross-company code payment scenario we are facing in our current S/4HANA implementation for a client in Qatar.

The Business Requirement

We have 5 company codes (1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 5000). Frequently, a payment needs to be made for an invoice sitting in one company code (e.g., 2000), but the funds will actually be disbursed from the bank account of another company code (e.g., 1000) due to balance availability.

The Current Configuration (The BRS Problem)

To achieve this, my senior configured the physical bank account (e.g., Al Rayyan Bank, Account No: 123456789) across all 5 company codes.

  • Because SAP does not allow duplicate bank account numbers for the same bank key, a workaround was used by appending the company code to the account number (e.g., 123456789-1000, 123456789-2000, etc.).

  • Main, Incoming, and Outgoing GLs were created in all company codes for this single physical bank account.

The Issue: This breaks our Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS). If a user selects the house bank in company code 2000 or 3000 to make a payment, the transactions sit in the GLs of those respective company codes. However, we receive only one consolidated physical bank statement from the bank. Reconciling one statement against GLs scattered across five different company codes is essentially impossible.

My Proposed Solution

Instead of duplicating the House Bank, I am planning to use the standard Cross-Company Code transaction process.

  1. House Bank Setup: Maintain the physical House Bank and its associated bank GLs only in the actual paying company code (e.g., 1000).

  2. OBYA Configuration: Configure OBYA (Cross-Company Code Clearing) between all necessary company code pairs using a dedicated Intercompany Receivable/Payable GL.

  3. Payment Process: When paying a CC 2000 invoice via F-53, the user starts in CC 1000, selects the 1000 Bank GL, and processes the open item for CC 2000. SAP automatically clears the invoice in 2000, posts the payment in 1000, and generates the OBYA intercompany postings.

The Complication: Document Splitting & Profit Centers

Here is where I need the most guidance. Document Splitting is active in our system.

  • Currently, Profit Centers are created 1:1 with Plants and are company-code specific (e.g., CC 1000 has PC 1100, CC 2000 has PC 2100).

  • Under my proposed OBYA solution, when paying a CC 2000 invoice from a CC 1000 bank account, Document Splitting will inherit the profit center from the vendor invoice (PC 2100) onto the CC 1000 bank line item.

  • To prevent system errors, I would need to extend all Profit Centers to all Company Codes. ### My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is the OBYA cross-company payment approach the standard way to resolve the BRS issue in this scenario?

  2. Regarding Document Splitting: Is extending all profit centers to all company codes the correct approach to handle the inheritance during cross-company payments? Will this create downstream reporting issues (e.g., trial balance or segment reporting conflicts), and if so, how do you typically mitigate them?

Any insights or guidance would be highly appreciated!

Best Regards,

Syamlal VS

SAP FICO Consultant

entry.title: Joule Work on RISE — do SAP GUI users need to move to Fiori, or only for the embedded Joule Assistan
entry.content:
We're a RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition) customer, and most users still work in classic SAP GUI, not Fiori.

My understanding is that the embedded Joule Assistant is surfaced inside the UI, so that would require Fiori — but Joule Work is a separate, standalone agentic workspace that executes against the backend via
APIs, independent of the user's transactional screen.

So my question is: for Joule Work specifically, can our SAP GUI users keep using GUI as-is, or do they need to migrate to Fiori?

Thanks!

entry.title: Cloud Application Programming For SAP Functional Consultant
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Is it required to Lean Cloud application programming language to every functional consultant in this Cloud and AI era.

If yes how to start and if basic understanding required could you please share the links so that can prepare and start the learning.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Solution for Attachment Replication from SAP Business Suite to sales cloud V2?
entry.content:
The standard Replicate Sales Order and Sales Quote from SAP S4HANA iFlow only covers header, products, prices, and parties "no attachments".

Is there a standard SAP iFlow/content package for replicating S/4 sales order/quote attachments to Sales Cloud V2 (similar to the old C4C AttachmentFolderReplicationRequest_Out scenario)? Or is a custom post-exit using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV → V2 AttachmentFolder the only way? or there is any other way to handle this integration

Has anyone built this in a live project?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Accrued Income (Results Analysis) not reversing during invoice posting
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are using Results Analysis (RA) for Project System WBS and Accrued Income (POCI/Unbilled Revenue) is being posted correctly during period-end settlement.

However, when we post the customer invoice, the previously posted Accrued Income is not getting reversed or cleared automatically.

SAP incident guidance below:

Due to this, Accrued Income balance remains open in the Balance Sheet even after billing.

As per expected standard behavior, RA Accrued Income should be adjusted or reversed during billing.

Kindly guide me any additional setup is required for automatic reversal during invoice posting

 

Thanks & Regards,

sethu

entry.title: Why 2026 Is the Tipping Point for Finance Transformation – Blog Post
entry.content:
Why 2026 Is the Tipping Point for Finance Transformation – Blog Post

Published:  July 2nd,, 2026

Author:  Christine Diaz, SAP Global Product Marketing

There’s a quiet shift happening inside finance organizations—and it’s accelerating.

For many years, finance teams have been asked to do more: close faster, report more accurately, support strategy, ensure compliance, and now—deliver predictive insight. But the underlying systems powering finance haven’t kept up. Many organizations are still operating across fragmented ERP landscapes, stitched together with manual processes and delayed reporting cycles.

 

The result? Finance is often reactive when it needs to be predictive. In 2026, that gap is no longer sustainable.

As such, Central Finance is emerging not just as a technical solution—but as a strategic enabler of modern enterprise performance.

Read more:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2026/03/0aa1454c-487f-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Please click on the links below to find out more about our Central Finance Product:
https://www.sap.com/products/financial-management/central-finance.html
 

Upcoming Central Finance Events:
https://events.sap.com/eur-centralfinance-and-beyond-2026/en_us/home.html
https://events.sap.com/us-sap-central-finance-and-beyond-conference-2026/en_us/home.html

entry.title: VLPOD Message RW033
entry.content:
While running transaction code VLPOD, the system generates error message RW033.

We have checked the relevant SAP Note and confirmed that no user exit exists in our system.

Could you please help investigate this issue?

Thank you.

entry.title: Product Costing for Group Valuation - Price control in legal and group valuation view
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have a question regarding Group Valuation in S/4HANA Private Cloud.

Our setup is:

  • 2 company codes (1010 and 1020) in one common controlling area
  • Both manufacture finished products
  • Standard costing (BEG) is used (Actual Costing 33Q is not activated and also no UPA is in place)
  • IC sales from 1020 to 1010
  • Two costing variants in place:
    • Legal valuation (incl. IC markup)
    • Group valuation (w/o IC markup)
  • Both variants update the Standard Price in different valuation variants
  • Approach: Multiple valuation in single ledger (0L)
    - Curr. Type 10 --> Currency: Comp. Code Curr. / Valuation view: legal
    - Curr. Type 30 --> Currency: Group Curr. / Valuation view: legal
    - Curr. Type 31 --> Currency: Group Curr. / Valuation view: Group

So in Material Master data, we are able to see Standard Price for Product from legal view as well as from group valuation view.

The issue is related to Price Control:
When Comp. Code 1010 purchases materials (FINI) from 1020, Price Control will be V (moving average price). Since Price Control is defined only once, it applies to all valuation views. Consequently, the Mov. Avg. Price incl. IC markup, is also reflected in the Group Valuation column in Material Master.
However, for Group Valuation we would expect the inventory valuation to be based on the group standard cost (without intercompany markup), while Legal Valuation should continue to use the moving average price.

My concern is that otherwise profitability reporting (e.g. Margin Analysis) will not show the correct contribution margin from a group valuation perspective.

Is this the expected system behavior? If so, how do customers typically handle this requirement? Is there a recommended setup to combine Moving Average Price for Legal Valuation with Standard Cost for Group Valuation?

Any insights or best practices would be highly appreciated.
Thanks in advance and best regards,

Max

entry.title: Purchase Order Workflow re-triggered - when delivery completed indicator is unchecked
entry.content: Purchase Order Workflow re-triggered - when delivery completed indicator is unchecked

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2023 Integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on integrating SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2023 with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) and would appreciate your guidance on the recommended integration approach.

I have the following questions:

Q1. Is SAP CPI mandatory for integrating SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise with SAP SFM?

Q2. Is the sap-sfm-integration-accelerator package mandatory for integrating SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise with SAP SFM, or is it optional?

Q3. The SAP SFM 'Manage Source Systems' app lists SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition as a supported source type for CDS-based replication. Does this same replication and extension framework (Manage Replications + Configure Extensions for Replications) also apply to On-Premise 2023, or does On-Premise require a different integration path?

Q4. How can transactional data be imported into SAP SFM? If I want to route a standard CDS view to a custom CDS view during the replication process, where can this mapping or redirection be configured?

Q5. What is the BADI for implementing customer-specific rules for MD and TD in SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise? In SAP SFM, the "Configure Extensions for Replications" app only exposes a CDS View option for extensibility, and no BADI option is available. For scenarios where a CDS view is not sufficient for MD/TD, is there a supported BADI or another enhancement framework for implementing customer-specific MD and TD logic in this integration with SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise? If so, where is it exposed and how can it be implemented?

Thank you for taking the time to read our questions. We greatly appreciate any guidance, suggestions, or experiences you can share.

Regards,
Shiva

entry.title: SAP IAG PAM – How Does Fiori Launchpad (/UI2/FLP) Work Within a Privileged Access in S/4HANA PCE?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are implementing SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG) Privileged Access Management (PAM) with SAP S/4HANA PCE 2023

I would like to understand the Fiori use case for Privileged Access IDs.

  • A user launches a PAM session (SIAG_PAM_LAUNCH_PAD) using a Privileged Access ID and then wants to perform activities using the SAP Fiori Launchpad (/UI2/FLP) instead of SAP GUI.

My questions are:

  • If the user accesses /UI2/FLP during an active PAM session, will the Fiori Launchpad run with the Privileged Access ID's authorizations or continue to use the physical user's SSO identity?
  • Is there a SAP-supported approach for using Fiori applications under a PAM ID?
  • If the Privileged Access ID has Fiori business roles assigned, can those roles be utilized through the PAM session?
  • Are there any additional configurations or best practices required to enable privileged activities in Fiori using SAP IAG PAM?

We are trying to understand the recommended approach for users who need to perform privileged activities through Fiori applications rather than SAP GUI transactions. Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Best Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: SAP IAG PAM – How Does Fiori Launchpad (/UI2/FLP) Work Within a Privileged Access in S/4HANA PCE?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are implementing SAP IAG PAM with SAP S/4HANA PCE 2023.

A user launches a PAM session using SIAG_PAM_LAUNCH_PAD and then wants to access the Fiori Launchpad (/UI2/FLP) instead of SAP GUI.

Could you please clarify:

  • Will Fiori run with the PAM ID authorizations or the physical user's SSO identity?
  • Is there a SAP-supported way to use Fiori applications under a PAM ID?
  • If Fiori business roles are assigned to the PAM ID, can they be used during the PAM session?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,
Vijay
